meta.title: Warehouse Racking & MHE Safety Assessment — Midlands DC, APR pallet racking, bays A1–A48
meta.subtitle: EN 15635 inspection regime · SEMA RAG remedial priority · engineered MHE/pedestrian segregation
meta.author: HSE Logistics & Transport
meta.deid_notice: Personnel and the prior struck-by incident are referred to by role label only. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This assessment covers the 8 m adjustable-pallet-racking (APR) installation at the Midlands DC, bays A1–A48, and the goods-in marshalling traffic layout. Controls are led by the SWL-rated configuration, the BS EN 15635 inspection regime, and engineered MHE/pedestrian segregation — not by 'inspect carefully' or hi-vis. Two SEMA Red-band frame buckles (bays A12 and A18) require immediate off-load and isolation. The >=12-monthly expert inspection is overdue and the SWL load notices for bays A25–A48 were not supplied ([GAP]). Goods-in is currently managed by hi-vis alone with no engineered segregation, which is pushed up the hierarchy to physical barriers and a one-way route.

rows: [GAP] — request SWL load notice / rack-design drawing; SWL not assumed
sections.caption: Racking configuration vs displayed SWL — an unsupplied SWL is [GAP], never assumed

items: PRRS appointment: Warehouse Operations Lead (role) — appointed and recorded
Weekly visual inspection: in place, logged by the shift team leader (role)
>=12-monthly expert inspection: OVERDUE — last documented expert inspection >14 months ago ([GAP] on the current report; high-priority finding)

rows.title: Bay A12 — upright frame buckle beyond tolerance (SEMA Red)
rows.description: SEMA Red — critical. Immediately off-load and isolate the A12 run; do not reload until repaired and re-inspected by the expert inspector. Not down-rated to 'monitor'.
rows.evidence: Weekly visual log, week 24; tolerance reference [GAP] pending the rack-design drawing

rows.title: Bay A18 — diagonal brace impact damage beyond tolerance (SEMA Red)
rows.description: SEMA Red — critical. Immediate off-load + isolate the A18 run; repair before reuse.

rows.title: Bay A07 — minor footplate deformation within tolerance (SEMA Amber)
rows.description: SEMA Amber — exceeds routine tolerance: repair/replace within 4 weeks and auto-escalate to Red if not actioned. The Amber off-load nuance is confirmed by the SEMA-approved inspector for this site.

sections.text: Goods-in marshalling is shared by counterbalance forklifts and pedestrian pickers and is currently controlled by hi-vis vests and a 'look out for forklifts' briefing only — no engineered segregation. This is flagged and pushed up the hierarchy: a physical barrier line, a one-way forklift route, and a marked + protected pedestrian walkway with a controlled crossing are required before hi-vis is treated as anything but a residual control (KB-REG-MHE-PIT / HSG136). Operator competence and pre-use truck inspection are evidenced for all four operators (role labels).

rows.control: Off-load + isolate the A12 and A18 Red-band runs; recalculate before any reconfiguration
Install a physical barrier line + one-way forklift route + protected pedestrian walkway/crossing in goods-in
Reinstate the >=12-monthly expert inspection and obtain the A25–A48 SWL load notices
Hi-vis + crossing discipline as a residual supplement to the engineered segregation

rows.action: Off-load + isolate bays A12 and A18 (SEMA Red)
Obtain SWL load notices / rack-design drawing for bays A25–A48 ([GAP] closure)
Install engineered goods-in segregation (barrier + one-way route + protected walkway)
Repair the A07 Amber footplate within 4 weeks before auto-escalation

sections.text: Any SEMA Red-band finding triggers an IMMEDIATE off-load + isolate of the affected run — it is never down-rated to 'monitor'. Amber repairs within 4 weeks and auto-escalates to Red. No SWL, tolerance, or inspection interval is assumed: every unsupplied value is a [GAP].

items: BS EN 15635:2008 + SEMA Code of Practice (racking inspection regime + RAG action map)
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.178 / PUWER 1998 / HSE ACOP L117 / HSG136 (MHE + engineered segregation)
India (if in scope): Motor Transport Workers Act 1961 / Factories Act via hse-india — state detection, [GAP], no national form
